mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment remains below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of observations during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings above 5.0% trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >65% of high-inflation months, or the threshold fails to separate positive from negative return regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates robust economic activity and wage growth, which supports corporate revenue expansion and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 2-year rolling window, or payroll growth correlates negatively with forward returns during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with elevated inflation indicates demand-pull price pressures that force monetary tightening, creating stagflation risks that suppress S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ growth > 2.0% and CPIAUCSL YoY > 4.0%
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive forward returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from independent series effects.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals manufacturing contraction and weakening supply-side momentum, which reduces earnings forecasts and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of contraction months, or the signal loses predictive power when monetary policy is accommodative.
